title: Interactive Walkthroughs
text_content:
CentenarianOS includes interactive walkthroughs for every major module — guided step-by-step tours that highlight exactly what to click and explain what each part of the interface does. Think of them as a personal tour guide built into the app.

---

### How Walkthroughs Work

When you first visit a module, a **Module Picker** modal offers to start a guided tour. Select the module you want to learn, and the **Tour Overlay** activates. A floating card appears with:

- **Step title and description** — what you're looking at and what it does
- **Progress bar** — how far through the tour you are
- **Next / Skip / Exit** buttons — advance, skip a step, or leave the tour

The overlay highlights the relevant UI element by scrolling it into view and adding a glowing ring around it. This makes it easy to see exactly what the tour is describing.

---

### Available Tours

Interactive tours cover:

- **Planner** — roadmaps, goals, milestones, and daily task views
- **Finance** — accounts, transactions, budgets, and bank linking
- **Travel** — vehicles, trips, fuel logs, and maintenance
- **Health Metrics** — core metrics, enrichment tier, and wearable sync
- **Workouts & Exercises** — templates, logs, exercise library, Nomad OS
- **Equipment** — asset tracking, valuations, and categories
- **Academy** — course browsing, enrollment, CYOA navigation
- **Data Hub** — import/export for all modules
- **Contractor (JobHub)** — jobs, time entries, invoices, rate cards, venues
- **Lister (CrewOps)** — job listing, roster, assignments, messaging

---

### Re-Taking Tours

Dismissed a tour or want a refresher? Go to **Settings > Module Tours**. You'll see every tour with its status (Completed, In Progress, or Not Started). Click **Restart** to reset any tour and run through it again from step 1.

On the contractor dashboard, Module Tours appears in the contractor settings page.

---

### Feature Pages & Demo Login

You can also explore modules before signing up. Visit **/features/contractor** or **/features/lister** to see detailed feature descriptions. Each page includes a **Try Demo Login** button that drops you into a demo account with pre-loaded sample data so you can explore the actual interface risk-free.

---

### Offline Access

Once you've loaded a tour or feature page, it's cached for offline access. If you lose connectivity, you can still browse tours and text-based content you've previously visited.
